title: Database Proof
purpose: Verify persisted state through database reads or fixtures without confusing local, proxy, and direct evidence.
trigger: Run when a task claims persistence, projection, migration, sync cursor, or readback behavior.
allowedActions: run read-only queries against approved databases
record row counts or selected redacted fields
forbiddenActions: dump private customer data into artifacts
claim pre/prod direct proof from a local fixture
bypass migrations or edit generated database state manually
gates: schema version or migration state is recorded
expected persisted field is observed or blocked

evidence.direct: Approved target database was queried read-only for the target persisted state.
evidence.proxy: Projection, export, or API readback implies database state but direct query was unavailable.
evidence.local: Local fixture, unit database, or migration test only.
evidence.blocked: Database access unavailable, query unsafe, schema ambiguous, or privacy constraints block inspection.

escalation: Ask before touching production or customer data.
Stop if proof requires writes to a database.
Escalate when privacy rules prevent enough evidence from being recorded.
